Question: Evaluate $$\underset{x \rightarrow - \infty}{\text{lim}} \left(\right. 3 + \frac{4}{x} \left.\right)$$ and $$\underset{x \rightarrow \infty}{\text{lim}} \left(\right. 3 + \frac{4}{x} \left.\right) .$$ Determine the horizontal asymptotes of $$f ( x ) = 3 + \frac{4}{x} ,$$ if any.
Answer: Both limits are $$3 .$$ The line $$y = 3$$ is a horizontal asymptote.

Question: Use the formal definition of limit at infinity to prove that $$\underset{x \rightarrow \infty}{\text{lim}} \left(\right. 3 - \frac{1}{x^{2}} \left.\right) = 3 .$$
Answer: Let $$\varepsilon > 0 .$$ Let $$N = \frac{1}{\sqrt{\varepsilon}} .$$ Therefore, for all $$x > N ,$$ we have $$| 3 - \frac{1}{x^{2}} - 3 | = \frac{1}{x^{2}} < \frac{1}{N^{2}} = \varepsilon$$ Therefore, $$\underset{x \rightarrow \infty}{\text{lim}} \left(\right. 3 - 1 / x^{2} \left.\right) = 3 .$$

Question: Use the formal definition of infinite limit at infinity to prove that $$\underset{x \rightarrow \infty}{\text{lim}} 3 x^{2} = \infty .$$
Answer: Let $$M > 0 .$$ Let $$N = \sqrt{\frac{M}{3}} .$$ Then, for all $$x > N ,$$ we have $$3 x^{2} > 3 N^{2} = 3 ( \sqrt{\frac{M}{3}} )^{2} ^{2} = \frac{3 M}{3} = M$$

Question: Let $$f ( x ) = -3 x^{4} .$$ Find $$\underset{x \rightarrow \infty}{\text{lim}} f \left(\right. x \left.\right) .$$

Evaluate $$\underset{x \rightarrow \pm \infty}{\text{lim}} \frac{3 x^{2} + 2 x - 1}{5 x^{2} - 4 x + 7}$$ and use these limits to determine the end behavior of $$f ( x ) = \frac{3 x^{2} + 2 x - 1}{5 x^{2} - 4 x + 7} .$$
Find the limits as $$x \vec{} \infty$$ and $$x \vec{} \text{unicode[:-]} \infty$$ for $$f ( x ) = \frac{( 3 e^{x} - 4 )}{( 5 e^{x} + 2 )} .$$
Sketch a graph of $$f ( x ) = ( x - 1 )^{3} ( x + 2 ) .$$
Sketch a graph of $$f ( x ) = \frac{( 3 x + 5 )}{( 8 + 4 x )} .$$
Find the oblique asymptote for $$f ( x ) = \frac{( 3 x^{3} - 2 x + 1 )}{( 2 x^{2} - 4 )} .$$
Consider the function $$f ( x ) = 5 - x^{2 \text{/} 3} .$$ Determine the point on the graph where a cusp is located. Determine the end behavior of $$f .$$
Answer: The function $$f$$ has a cusp at $$( 0 , 5 )$$ $$\underset{x \rightarrow 0^{-}}{\text{lim}} f^{'} \left(\right. x \left.\right) = \infty ,$$ $$\underset{x \rightarrow 0^{+}}{\text{lim}} f^{'} \left(\right. x \left.\right) = - \infty .$$ For end behavior, $$\underset{x \rightarrow \pm \infty}{\text{lim}} f \left(\right. x \left.\right) = - \infty .$$
